topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud, the subject line stark and unforgiving: "Party Cancellation." My fingers hovered over the screen, a strange, cold knot tightening in my chest. I reread the message, the words blurring slightly. Sarah’s party. All the planning, the coordinating, the careful selection of decorations—it was all dissolving into this single, brutal notification. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips, a sound that seemed too loud in the quiet apartment. I leaned back in my chair, the springs groaning under my weight, and stared blankly at the wall, tracing patterns in the dust motes dancing in the afternoon light. A wave of profound disappointment washed over me, leaving a hollow ache just beneath my ribs.

[story 2]

I kept checking my phone, half-expecting a retraction, a typo, anything to suggest this was a mistake. But the silence from the organizers was absolute. When the final confirmation came through, the air seemed to thin around me. My shoulders slumped involuntarily, the tension draining out of me so quickly I felt suddenly weak. I walked over to the window, pulling the curtain aside just enough to see the mundane street below. Every detail of the afternoon—the planned laughter, the carefully chosen music, the shared anticipation—felt suddenly tainted, diminished. I pressed my palms against the cool glass, feeling a tightness spread through my chest, a heavy, sinking feeling that made breathing shallow.

[story 3]

"No," I whispered to the empty room, the sound barely audible. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between the sofa and the bookshelf. My movements were jerky, uncoordinated, as if my limbs were weighted with lead. The reality of the situation settled in, heavy and undeniable. I picked up a forgotten glass, turning it over and over in my hands, the crystal feeling cold and unforgiving. A tremor ran through my hands, and I squeezed my eyes shut, trying to physically hold back the sudden urge to just collapse onto the floor. The realization that this event, so central to the coming weeks, was simply gone, left me feeling utterly adrift.
